steps.title: End the first sentence
steps.content: The first part says the veins provide structural support. The next part begins a participial phrase, decreasing in thickness from the center to the edges, and then gives a full main clause about what the veins allow.

steps.title: Reject run-on structures
steps.content: Choice B creates a garbled phrase and leaves another clause unseparated. Choice C and Choice D use commas that cannot properly separate the completed support statement from the next independent clause. A period after support cleanly ends the first sentence and lets the participial phrase modify these veins. The answer is A.
